data.title: Western snow drought weighs on Vail Resorts' outlook
data.description:
Reflecting below-average snowfall and unseasonably warm weather, Vail Resorts (MTN) experienced weaker early-season skier visitation leading management to warn full-year resort reported EBITDA to fall just below the low end of $842M to $898M guidance, contingent on a return to normal Rocky Mountain conditions by mid-February.

The company also warned that the weather impact could further weigh on guidance if conditions do not return to normal for the remainder of the 2025/2026 ski season.

Accordingly, Vail Resorts (MTN) shares opened as much as 5% lower on Thursday before snapping back to unchanged from Wednesday’s close.    

From the beginning of the ski season to January 4, skier visits were down 20% from the same period last year, while total lift revenue was down 1.8%. The drop in visits also resulted in a 15% drop in ski school and dining revenue and a 6% decline in retail/rental revenue for the company’s North American ski area store locations.

“We experienced one of the worst early season snowfalls in the western U.S. in over 30 years, which limited our ability to open terrain and negatively impacted visitation and ancillary spending for both local and destination guests during the period,” said Vail Resorts CEO Rob Katz.

Snowfall at the company’s western U.S. resorts was half of the 30-year average, while snowfall in the Rockies was down 60% versus the 30-year average, preventing the company from opening all but 11% of total terrain in December.

Conditions in Tahoe were “near historic lows through mid-December,” while Whistler in British Columbia also had a slower start, although experienced some improvement over the December holiday period.

Fortunately, significant snowfall in the east that provided favorable skiing conditions helped offset some of the impact from the dearth of snow out west.

data.title: MetaVia prices $8.1M underwritten public offering
data.description:
• MetaVia (NASDAQ:MTVA) on Thursday  stated that it priced an underwritten public offering of common stock and Series C and D warrants for gross proceeds of about $8.1M, with underwriters granted a 45-day option to purchase up to an additional 0.39M common shares, 0.59M Series C warrants, and 0.59 Series D warrants.
  • The offering consists of 2.61M common shares, 3.92M Series C warrants, and 3.92M Series D warrants at a combined price of $3.10 per share.
  • Series C warrants are immediately exercisable at $3.10, expiring in five years, and Series D warrants at $3.10, expiring in two years.
  • Ladenburg Thalmann & Co. Inc. is acting as sole book-running manager in connection with the underwritten public offering.
  • Full exercise of pre-funded Series C and D warrants could yield up to $24M in gross proceeds.
  • Proceeds will fund working capital, corporate purposes, and the clinical development of DA-1726 obesity trials.
  • The offering is expected to close on or about Jan. 16, 2026, subject to customary conditions.
  • Shares -41.88%.

data.title: STAAR Surgical, Broadwood Partners enter cooperation pact
data.description:
STAAR Surgical (STAA) said that Neal C. Bradsher and Richard T. LeBuhn of Broadwood Partners and Christopher Wang of Yunqi Capital have joined its board under a cooperation pact with Broadwood, which owns 31% of STAAR. Shares of STAAR fell 3.3%. 

STAAR (STAA) CEO Stephen Farrell has stepped down from the board, but will remain CEO until the end of the month, according to a statement on Thursday.  STAAR Chair Elizabeth Yeu also stepped down from the board. 

The STAAR (STAA) board expanded its size from six members to seven. The renewed board intends to make additional announcements regarding STAAR’s next chair and CEO in the near term. 

The board changes come after Alcon (ALC) last week terminated its purchase of STAAR (STAA) after failing to win enough votes from shareholders for the deal amid pressure from Broadwood and Yunqi to vote against the transacton.

data.title: Market sentiment turns more bullish in mid-January, shows latest AAII survey
data.description:
Market sentiment among individual investors turned more optimistic in mid-January, according to the latest survey from the American Association of Individual Investors (AAII).

Bullish sentiment rose to 49.5% for the week ended January 14, up sharply from 42.5% in the prior week, while bearish sentiment stood at 28.2%, down from 30.0% a week earlier.

U.S. equities traded unevenly during the week as investors weighed a heavy flow of economic data, shifting inflation signals, and rising policy uncertainty. Stocks moved between gains and losses after labor market and inflation reports delivered mixed signals, while technology shares faced pressure at points during the week.

Volatility increased following reports related to a probe involving Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell, though sentiment later steadied as analysts and policymakers expressed confidence in the Fed’s independence.

Global markets were mixed. European stocks hovered near record highs but struggled for direction amid softer trade and inflation data, while Asian markets posted modest gains as investors assessed mixed U.S. data, China-related developments, and ongoing geopolitical risks. Attention also turned to the start of the U.S. earnings season, with analysts highlighting uneven earnings momentum across sectors and investors watching corporate guidance closely.

According to AAII, neutral sentiment, or expectations that stock prices will stay essentially unchanged over the next six months, eased to 22.3% from 27.5% a week earlier.

The survey has been conducted by the American Association of Individual Investors since 1987, in which it asks respondents for their thoughts on where the market is heading in the next six months.

S&P 500 Tracking Funds: (MUTF:FXAIX), (MUTF:VFIAX), (MUTF:VFFSX), (MUTF:SWPPX), (NYSEARCA:SPY), (NYSEARCA:VOO), (NYSEARCA:IVV), (NYSEARCA:RSP), (NYSEARCA:SSO), (NYSEARCA:SH), (NYSEARCA:SDS) and (NYSEARCA:SPXU).

data.title: KLA, Lam Research receive upgrades on WFE acceleration: Wells Fargo
data.description:
Wells Fargo upgraded both KLA (KLAC) and Lam Research (LRCX) to Overweight from Equal-weight due to a likely acceleration in the wafer fabrication equipment market.

Applied Materials (AMAT) remains Wells Fargo's "top pick" among the semiconductor equipment sector.

Lam Research and KLA were up 9% and 6%, respectively, during early market trading on Thursday. Applied Materials was up 7.5%.

"Although semi cap is now a consensus long & has outperformed YTD, avg +20% vs. Philadelphia Semiconductor Index (SOX) +9%, we think the group can continue to work as we expect demand data points to suggest a further tightening of logic & memory supply/demand dynamics supportive of a WFE acceleration in 2027," said Wells Fargo analysts, led by Aaron Rakers, in a Thursday investor report. "We increase our WFE estimate by an average of 10% & raise C2026 & C2027 estimates to above Street."

Wells Fargo increased its price target on KLA to $1,600 from $1,250 and raised Lam's to $250 from $145.

"We are upgrading KLA to Overweight from Equal Weight as we think the company can continue to drive WFE outperformance by 2 nm momentum," Rakers noted. "We expect positive 2 nm demand dynamics to continue in 2026, in which we expect inspection sample rates to remain elevated as the industry begins to introduce BSPD."

Meanwhile, Lam is expected to outperform due to memory supply and demand dynamics. 

"While we expect Lam to reiterate that its estimated $40B of incremental spending on NAND to upgrade >2XXL will play out over multiple years, increasing tightness in the NAND industry will leave it focused on an acceleration in 2026/2027," Rakers added.

Earlier this week, Stifel said it expects WFE spending to increase by 10% to 15% in 2026, which equates to $10B to $15B in year-over-year growth. The gains will be led by advanced foundry/logic and DRAM.

data.title: Wall Street rallies as chip stocks climb and Trump pauses critical mineral tariffs
data.description:
Wall Street advanced on Thursday as investors reacted positively to President Donald Trump’s decision to delay tariffs on critical minerals, alongside his assurance that he does not intend to remove Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell.

Leading the way higher was the tech-focused Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) which advanced 0.9%. At the same time, the benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) climbed 0.6%, and the blue-chip Dow (DJI) was higher by 0.2%. 

From a sector vantage point, eight of the S&P 500’s 11 sectors were in positive trading territory, with Info Tech leading the charge. Going in the other direction, Energy has suffered the most so far as oil prices (CL1:COM) are down 4%. 

Treasury yields edged higher across the curve as investors sold bonds. The shorter-end U.S. 2-year Treasury yield (US2Y) moved up 3 basis points to 3.55%, while the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) ticked up 1 basis point to 4.14%. 

On the economic calendar front, initial jobless claims dipped by 9K to 198K for the week ended Jan. 10, which was lower than the 215K forecasted level. Also the Empire State Manufacturing Index came in at 7.7 versus the 1.0 consensus level according to data released by the Federal Reserve Bank of New York. 

As for stocks that are on the move, shares of Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company Limited (TSM) have popped up 3.7%, and Boston Scientific (BSX) fell 4.8%.

data.title: Canadian Solar wins U.S. patent dispute against Maxeon
data.description:
Canadian Solar (CSIQ) +2.6% pre-market Thursday after saying it won a favorable ruling in its patent dispute with Maxeon Solar Technologies (MAXN) from the Patent Trial and Appeal Board of the U.S. Patent and Trademark Office.

Canadian Solar (CSIQ) said the board's ruling held that all claims asserted by Maxeon (MAXN) related to alleged infringement of patents at issue in the federal court litigation are invalid.

Canadian Solar (CSIQ) denied allegations of patent infringement by Singapore-based Maxeon (MAXN) related to the company's TOPCon solar cell technology.

"This victory not only reflects the fairness and soundness of the international intellectual property protection framework, but also demonstrates Canadian Solar's solid technological foundation and its mature legal capabilities," the company said.

data.title: Allstate estimates sequential fall in Q4 catastrophe losses
data.description:
Allstate (ALL) on Thursday estimated December catastrophe losses at $80M, or $64M after-tax, compared to November's $46M, or $36M after-tax.

However, fourth-quarter catastrophe losses are expected to fall to $209M, or $165M after-tax, compared to $558M in Q3.

Shares were +0.50% pre-market to $197.00.

Protection policies in force increased by 0.2% in December to 38.28M from 38.21M in November.

data.title: ImmunityBio gains as Anktiva powers over 400% Q4 sales growth
data.description:
ImmunityBio (IBRX) shares traded higher on Thursday after the company announced that, based on preliminary financials, its net product revenue for Q4 ending December 31, 2025, reached nearly $38.3M, indicating ~431% YoY growth driven by its cancer therapy Anktiva.

Compared to $31.8M recorded in Q3 2025, Q4 indicated a 20% sequential sales growth, the San Diego, California-based biotech said, adding that its full-year preliminary net product revenue reached nearly $113M, implying ~700% YoY growth.

Approved in multiple countries, including the U.S. and the U.K., as a bladder cancer therapy, Anktiva posted 750% unit sales volume growth last year compared to 2024, according to ImmunityBio (IBRX).

Additionally, the company reported $242.8M in cash, cash equivalents, and marketable securities as of the end of last year and highlighted the recent approvals of Anktiva for non-small cell lung cancer and bladder cancer in Saudi Arabia.

data.title: Stuhini Cxploration changes corporate name to EraNova Metals
data.description:
• Stuhini Exploration (STU:CA) said on Thursday that it will officially change its name to EraNova Metals with a new ticker symbol 'NOVA', effective January 19, 2026.
  • The name change aligns with EraNova's forward-looking strategy to advance exploration and development across the Ruby Creek Project- which hosts a large molybdenum resource alongside high-grade silver, gold, and emerging copper-gold porphyry targets, the company said.
  • The CUSIP and ISIN numbers for the common shares have also been changed to 29478G106 and CA29478G1063, respectively.
  • Source: Press Release
